verses.text: The LORD: on the first day of the first month rear up the tabernacle. Put in the ark; cover with the vail. Bring in the table. Bring in the candlestick, light lamps. Set the golden altar before the ark; set the door hanging. Set the brass altar before the door; laver with water. Set up the court; hang the gate. Guidance: God names the order: ark first, then food, light, incense, sacrifice, water, enclosure. The room is set inside-out, from His throne outward. Holiness furnishes the world.

Guidance: God names the order: ark first, then food, light, incense, sacrifice, water, enclosure. The room is set inside-out, from His throne outward. Holiness furnishes the world.

Take the anointing oil, anoint the tabernacle and all therein — sanctify it and its vessels; most holy. Anoint the altar of burnt offering and its vessels — sanctify it; most holy. Anoint the laver and foot, sanctify it. Guidance: Oil goes on every piece — even the brass altar, even the laver. Nothing in God's house is half-holy. The same oil that anoints the priest sanctifies the furniture; they share one consecration.

Take the anointing oil, anoint the tabernacle and all therein — sanctify it and its vessels; most holy. Anoint the altar of burnt offering and its vessels — sanctify it; most holy. Anoint the laver and foot, sanctify it. Guidance: Oil goes on every piece — even the brass altar, even the laver. Nothing in God's house is half-holy. The same oil that anoints the priest sanctifies the furniture; they share one consecration.

Anoint the laver and foot, sanctify it. Guidance: Oil goes on every piece — even the brass altar, even the laver. Nothing in God's house is half-holy. The same oil that anoints the priest sanctifies the furniture; they share one consecration.

Bring Aaron and his sons to the tabernacle door; wash them. Put Aaron in the holy garments; anoint and sanctify him to minister to Me. Bring his sons; clothe with coats; anoint them as their father. Their anointing shall be an everlasting priesthood throughout generations. Guidance: Washed, clothed, anointed. The priests are made the same way the tabernacle is made. The body and the building are sanctified by the same hand. God prepares both at once.

Moses did all the LORD commanded. On the first day of the first month in the second year, the tabernacle was reared up. Moses reared it — sockets, boards, bars, pillars. Spread abroad the tent, put the covering above — as the LORD commanded. Guidance: One year after Passover, the tabernacle stands. A new year began with Egypt's plague; this new year begins with God's house. Time itself now turns around His dwelling.

Took the testimony, put it into the ark, set the staves, put the mercy seat above. Brought the ark into the tabernacle, set up the vail of covering, covered the ark of testimony — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: The two tables — God's own writing — go inside the ark. Mercy seat above; vail in front. The Word and the throne are at the center of God's house, where they have always belonged.

Took the testimony, put it into the ark, set the staves, put the mercy seat above. Brought the ark into the tabernacle, set up the vail of covering, covered the ark of testimony — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: The two tables — God's own writing — go inside the ark. Mercy seat above; vail in front. The Word and the throne are at the center of God's house, where they have always belonged.

Put the table in the tent of the congregation on the north side, outside the vail. Set the bread in order before the LORD — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Twelve loaves on the table on the north side. The bread is set before the LORD, but the LORD never eats it; the priests do. God's table feeds those who serve in His presence.

Put the table in the tent of the congregation on the north side, outside the vail. Set the bread in order before the LORD — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Twelve loaves on the table on the north side. The bread is set before the LORD, but the LORD never eats it; the priests do. God's table feeds those who serve in His presence.

Put the candlestick in the tent of the congregation, over against the table on the south side. Lit the lamps before the LORD — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Light opposite bread, just as Jesus would later say I am the bread of life, and I am the light of the world. The two great I-AMs stand on either side of the holy place.

Put the candlestick in the tent of the congregation, over against the table on the south side. Lit the lamps before the LORD — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Light opposite bread, just as Jesus would later say I am the bread of life, and I am the light of the world. The two great I-AMs stand on either side of the holy place.

Put the golden altar in the tent of the congregation before the vail. Burnt sweet incense thereon — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Right before the vail, the golden altar burns. Incense rises and slips into the holy of holies. The first time it is lit, prayer goes up into God's room. The intercession has begun.

Put the golden altar in the tent of the congregation before the vail. Burnt sweet incense thereon — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Right before the vail, the golden altar burns. Incense rises and slips into the holy of holies. The first time it is lit, prayer goes up into God's room. The intercession has begun.

Set up the hanging at the door of the tabernacle. Put the altar of burnt offering by the door, offered upon it the burnt offering and the meat offering — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: The very first sacrifice ever offered on the new altar — Moses' hands, before Aaron has even begun his ministry. Approach to God always begins at this same place: an offering at the door.

Set up the hanging at the door of the tabernacle. Put the altar of burnt offering by the door, offered upon it the burnt offering and the meat offering — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: The very first sacrifice ever offered on the new altar — Moses' hands, before Aaron has even begun his ministry. Approach to God always begins at this same place: an offering at the door.

Set the laver between the tent and the altar; put water for washing. Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed hands and feet. When they went into the tent and when they came near the altar, they washed — as the LORD commanded. Guidance: Even the first priests wash before entering. Holy work never starts unwashed. The same God who washed Egypt out of Israel keeps washing the dust of the day off their hands.

Set the laver between the tent and the altar; put water for washing. Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed hands and feet. When they went into the tent and when they came near the altar, they washed — as the LORD commanded. Guidance: Even the first priests wash before entering. Holy work never starts unwashed. The same God who washed Egypt out of Israel keeps washing the dust of the day off their hands.

Set the laver between the tent and the altar; put water for washing. Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed hands and feet. He reared up the court round about the tabernacle and the altar, set up the hanging at the court gate. So Moses finished the work. Guidance: A fourth time in two chapters: finished. Genesis 2:2 said the same of creation. Moses' last act in Exodus is to close the gate of a finished world — Eden rebuilt in linen and gold.

A cloud covered the tent of the congregation; the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle. Moses could not enter because the cloud abode thereon, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle. Guidance: The same cloud that led Israel out of Egypt comes home. God moves into the house His people built. Even Moses, the friend who spoke face to face, cannot enter — the glory inside is greater than Sinai.

A cloud covered the tent of the congregation; the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle. Moses could not enter because the cloud abode thereon, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle. Guidance: The same cloud that led Israel out of Egypt comes home. God moves into the house His people built. Even Moses, the friend who spoke face to face, cannot enter — the glory inside is greater than Sinai.

When the cloud rose, Israel went onward in all their journeys. If it rose not, they journeyed not. The cloud of the LORD was on the tabernacle by day, and fire by night, before all Israel throughout their journeys. Guidance: Israel will not move until God moves. From Sinai on, the schedule belongs to the cloud. Cloud by day, fire by night — the same God in two forms, leading by what light Israel can bear. Exodus ends on the move with God in the middle.
